mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately compress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, which triggers short-term equity momentum as firms report margin expansion before supply constraints emerge.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle phases correlate with negative 5-day forward returns, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly translates to higher aggregate corporate profits, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL thresholds above 0.4% fail to predict negative returns, or equity markets rally on inflation surprises due to growth optimism, breaking the negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when PAYEMS growth decouples from UNRATE declines, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks that erode corporate margins and trigger negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases correlate with positive 5-day returns, or the signal fails to predict direction when inventory adjustments offset production drops.
